name: RABBIT-FILE-BUG-10
title: allocate_id can burn ID slot when commit_item fails or is never called
description: branch_ops.allocate_id() reserves a counter value and pushes counter.json BEFORE commit_item() writes the item.json. If commit_item is never called (process killed, exception in file-item.py between phases, network failure), the ID is permanently allocated but no item.json exists. Result: gaps in the ID sequence with no reclamation. Spec acknowledges allocate_id reads N writes N+1 commits but does not say what happens on partial failure. Suggested fix either merge allocate+commit into one atomic worktree session, or add idempotent recovery that detects a reserved-but-uncommitted ID on next allocate_id and reuses it.
